Orbit and clock combination of PPP-B2b, HAS and MADOCA-PPP at the

from PPP-B2b, HAS, and MADOCA-PPP, sampled at 5-minute intervals
for presentation clarity. Statistical results of satellite counts by
constellation are provided in Table 2. Overall, HAS provides corrections
for approximately 50 satellites, although occasional drops in this num-
ber were observed, likely due to network issues. In contrast, the number
of satellites for PPP-B2b and MADOCA-PPP remained relatively stable
throughout the test period, fluctuating around 18 and 69, respectively.

corrections from PPP-B2b, HAS, and MADOCA-PPP, assuming a
vacuum. With the exception of the clock correction for BDS-3

G. Chen et al. M e a s u r e m e n t 279 (2026) 121739
the PPP-B2b, HAS, and MADOCA-PPP services. Specifically, the precise
GPS orbits from MADOCA-PPP and HAS are sequentially used as
candidate reference orbits. The rotation parameters of the PPP-B2b or-
bits to these reference orbits are then estimated by orbit comparison.
Finally, this estimated correction is applied to both the GPS and BDS-3
precise orbit products of PPP-B2b.
2.2.2. Real-time orbit combination

3. Results
3.1. Products availability
Fig. 6illustrates the global distribution of available satellites for the
combined product generated by Wuhan Combination Center (WCC),
considering scenarios with (labeled as WCC(CJ)) and without (labeled as
WCC) the broadcast satellite visibility constraints of PPP-B2b and

G. Chen et al. M e a s u r e m e n t 279 (2026) 121739
orbits are 11.4 cm, 6.7 cm, and 7.1 cm, respectively.
For constellations supported by a single service, the combined
GLONASS orbit inherits the performance of MADOCA-PPP, with a 3D
RMS of 11.3 cm. The combined BDS-3 orbit, however, shows an
improvement over the original PPP-B2b product, a direct benefit of the
pre-combination rotational error correction. This is reflected in the 3D
RMS values for MEO satellites, which improve from 33.2 cm (PPP-B2b)
to 31.1 cm (combined), and for IGSO satellites, from 46.3 cm to 43.0 cm.

Fig. 13presents the RMS errors for the convergence phase of posi-
tioning, once again confirming that the WCC product improves posi-
tioning accuracy. This improvement is particularly significant when
compared to PPP-B2b and HAS. For the Group S1 stations, WCC reduces
the horizontal/vertical RMS errors of the PPP-B2b and HAS GPS-only
solutions, representing an average im
